// C2: Acting Components
instance GreenhouseActuatorController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Centralized controller for greenhouse environmental actuators (shading, ventilation) managed via virtualized VMs with pre-recorded actuation data. Uses ActiveMQ for real-time command propagation between physical and digital systems."
]

instance AirQualityActuatorManager : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Raspberry Pi-based central node managing air quality actuators (fans, filters) with AutomationML-defined control interfaces. Constrained by sensor limits and Azure cloud safety gateways."
]

instance GantryCraneMotorController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Embedded controller managing motorized mechanisms for precise positioning (linear actuators for puck transport) with ±1 mm precision via encoder feedback."
]

// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance GreenhouseTemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Low-cost temperature sensor integrated into greenhouse, sampling at ~1 Hz. Accuracy assumed to meet IoT sensor standards (±1°C)."
]

instance GreenhouseHumiditySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Low-cost humidity sensor in greenhouse, sampling at ~1 Hz. Accuracy assumed to meet IoT sensor standards."
]

instance GreenhouseLightSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Light intensity sensor in greenhouse, sampling at ~1 Hz. Accuracy assumed to meet IoT sensor standards."
]

instance AirQualityCO2Sensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensirion SCD30 CO₂ sensor on Raspberry Pi, sampling at ~5-10 Hz. Accuracy ±5% CO₂ measurement."
]

instance AirQualityTemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "DHT22 temperature sensor on Raspberry Pi, sampling at ~5-10 Hz. Accuracy ±5% temperature measurement."
]

instance AirQualityHumiditySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "DHT22 humidity sensor on Raspberry Pi, sampling at ~5-10 Hz. Accuracy ±5% humidity measurement."
]

instance GantryEncoderPositionSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Incremental encoder measuring gantry position with ±0.1 m accuracy. Sampling at ~1 kHz for actuator feedback."
]

instance GantryLoadCellSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Load cell measuring force with ±10 N accuracy. Sampling at ~1 kHz for structural validation."
]

instance GantryPuckProximitySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Proximity sensor detecting puck positions with high-resolution spatial distribution. Sampling at ~1 kHz for collision avoidance."
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance GreenhouseTemperatureDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom GreenhouseTemperatureSensor
]

instance GreenhouseHumidityDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom GreenhouseHumiditySensor
]

instance GreenhouseLightDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom GreenhouseLightSensor
]

instance AirQualityCO2DataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom AirQualityCO2Sensor
]

instance AirQualityTemperatureDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom AirQualityTemperatureSensor
]

instance AirQualityHumidityDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom AirQualityHumiditySensor
]

instance GantryPositionDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom GantryEncoderPositionSensor
]

instance GantryForceDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom GantryLoadCellSensor
]

instance GantryPuckStatusDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom GantryPuckProximitySensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance GreenhouseBehavioralModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Self-adaptive behavioral model for greenhouse system incorporating pre-recorded VM data and real-time ActiveMQ synchronization. Enables uniform adaptation across physical/digital components."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ActiveMQMessageBroker
]

instance AirQualitySimulationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "AutomationML-based model for indoor air quality simulation with runtime visualization and prediction workflows. Incorporates Raspberry Pi sensor data and Azure cloud analytics."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo AutomationMLSchema
]

instance GantryKinematicModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Geometric and behavioral model for lab-scale gantry crane motion control. Combines physical layout (container positions) with analytical force/position calculations."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EncoderFeedbackSystem
]

instance GreenhouseTimeSeriesData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time-series data storage for greenhouse operations (temperature, humidity, light) stored in InfluxDB with ~1 Hz sampling frequency."
    DTDFVocab:fromData GreenhouseTemperatureDataTransmitted, GreenhouseHumidityDataTransmitted, GreenhouseLightDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo InfluxDBTimeSeriesDB
]

instance AirQualityRealTimeData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Real-time sensor data aggregation for air quality management (CO₂, temperature, humidity) with ~5-10 Hz sampling frequency."
    DTDFVocab:fromData AirQualityCO2DataTransmitted, AirQualityTemperatureDataTransmitted, AirQualityHumidityDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo AzureIoTHub
]

instance GantryOperationalData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Historical operational data for gantry crane (position, force, trajectory) logged via TimescaleDB with sub-second sampling frequency."
    DTDFVocab:fromData GantryPositionDataTransmitted, GantryForceDataTransmitted, GantryPuckStatusDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo TimescaleDBDatabase
]

// C11: Enablers
instance ActiveMQMessageBroker : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "ActiveMQ message broker enabling real-time synchronization between physical greenhouse sensors and digital twin states. Facilitates event-driven updates with low-latency communication."
    DTDFVocab:enables GreenhouseBehavioralModel, GreenhouseTimeSeriesData
]

instance AzureIoTHub : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Azure IoT Hub for cloud-bound data transmission from Raspberry Pi sensors (CO₂, temperature, humidity) with 100 ms latency. Enables enterprise-wide interoperability via Azure Event Grid."
    DTDFVocab:enables AirQualityRealTimeData
]

instance InfluxDBTimeSeriesDB : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "InfluxDB time-series database for storing greenhouse sensor data with ~1 Hz sampling frequency. Enables real-time analytics and adaptive decision-making."
    DTDFVocab:enables GreenhouseTimeSeriesData
]

instance TimescaleDBDatabase : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "TimescaleDB database for storing gantry crane operational data with sub-second sampling frequency. Enables real-time validation and historical analysis."
    DTDFVocab:enables GantryOperationalData
]

instance AutomationMLSchema : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "AutomationML schema defining structured digital twin models for air quality management and construction engineering interoperability."
    DTDFVocab:enables AirQualitySimulationModel
]

instance EncoderFeedbackSystem : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Embedded system providing encoder feedback for precise gantry crane positioning with ±0.1 m accuracy. Enables continuous validation of motion trajectories."
    DTDFVocab:enables GantryKinematicModel
]

// C6: Services
instance RealTimeAdaptationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Self-adaptive service adjusting greenhouse digital twin parameters to match physical behavior via ActiveMQ event-driven loops. Implicit confidence measures based on data consistency."
    DTDFVocab:provides GreenhouseBehavioralAdaptationInsight
]

instance AirQualityVisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Azure-based runtime visualization service providing real-time dashboards for air quality operators. Enables operator intervention via Azure dashboards."
    DTDFVocab:provides AirQualityRuntimeVisualizationInsight
]

instance AirQualitySimulationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Predictive simulation service modeling virus spread/air quality trends using AutomationML and Azure ML. No explicit confidence metrics provided."
    DTDFVocab:provides AirQualitySimulationPredictionInsight
]

instance AirQualityPredictionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Azure ML-based forecasting service predicting air quality trends from historical sensor data. No formalized confidence thresholds."
    DTDFVocab:provides AirQualityTrendPredictionInsight
]

instance GantryContinuousValidationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Continuous validation service comparing physical and simulated gantry crane trajectories using Euclidean distance metrics. Enforces ±5 mm threshold for deviations."
    DTDFVocab:provides GantryTrajectoryValidationInsight
]

instance GantryFaultDetectionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time fault detection service identifying operational issues via force/position anomalies in gantry crane data."
    DTDFVocab:provides GantryOperationalFaultInsight
]

instance GantryPerformanceOptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Optimal control service improving gantry crane efficiency through motion strategy adjustments based on real-time sensor feedback."
    DTDFVocab:provides GantryEfficiencyOptimizationAction
]

instance GantryVisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Interactive 3D modeling service providing real-time and historical operational insights for researchers, engineers, and maintenance personnel."
    DTDFVocab:provides GantryPerformanceAnalyticsInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance GreenhouseBehavioralAdaptationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time insight generated by self-adaptive loops adjusting digital twin parameters to maintain consistency with physical greenhouse behavior."
]

instance AirQualityRuntimeVisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [

]

instance AirQualitySimulationPredictionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [

]

    base:desc "Forecasted air quality trends based on historical sensor data and Azure ML models."
]

]

    base:desc "Automatic optimization action improving gantry crane efficiency through adjusted motion strategies."

]

]

// C17: Environment
instance GreenhouseEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains GreenhouseActuatorController, GreenhouseTemperatureSensor, GreenhouseHumiditySensor, GreenhouseLightSensor, GreenhouseBehavioralModel, GreenhouseTimeSeriesData, ActiveMQMessageBroker
]

instance AirQualityEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains AirQualityActuatorManager, AirQualityCO2Sensor, AirQualityTemperatureSensor, AirQualityHumiditySensor, AirQualitySimulationModel, AirQualityRealTimeData, AzureIoTHub, AutomationMLSchema

instance GantryCraneEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains GantryCraneMotorController, GantryEncoderPositionSensor, GantryLoadCellSensor, GantryPuckProximitySensor, GantryKinematicModel, GantryOperationalData, TimescaleDBDatabase, EncoderFeedbackSystem

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "
The **digital-to-physical interaction** in the gantry crane system is managed through the **Control Application** and **Trajectory Generator Service**, which generate and execute commands to the **Physical Crane** via the **Physical Crane Interface**.

**Command Types**:
- **Trajectory Commands**: Optimal movement paths (no residual swinging) generated by the **Trajectory Generator Service** (kinematics-based).
- **Feedback Commands**: Real-time adjustments (e.g., emergency stops) triggered by the **Continuous Validation Service** if thresholds are breached.

**Control Message Formats**:
- Commands are transmitted via the **Physical Crane Interface** (Python program) to the crane’s controllers, likely using **industrial protocol** (e.g., PLC/SCADA-compatible commands).
- The **Human Machine Interface (HMI)** provides operator input (e.g., manual overrides) via the **Control Application**, which translates inputs into executable commands.

**Validation & Authentication**:
- The **Continuous Validation Service** validates trajectory adherence by comparing simulated vs. physical execution, notifying operators via the **Dashboard** if discrepancies exceed thresholds.
- **Conflict Resolution**: Operators can override automated commands via the HMI, with explicit feedback confirming command execution (e.g., acknowledgment messages in the **Logging Application**).

**Feedback Systems**:
- The **Continuous Validation Service** provides **distance metrics** (e.g., Euclidean error) as feedback to the **Trajectory Generator Service** for iterative refinement.
- The **Logging Application** records all commands and their outcomes for post-mortem analysis.

**Command Execution Confirmation**:
- The **Physical Crane Interface** logs successful command execution in the **Database**, ensuring auditability.
"
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "
  **GreenhouseDT**:
  - Synchronization between the physical greenhouse and its digital twin relies on **real-time behavioral adaptation** with **active feedback loops** between the physical and digital systems.
  - The system leverages **InfluxDB** for time-series data storage, implying **millisecond to second-scale synchronization** for sensor data ingestion and model updates.
  - **ActiveMQ message broker** facilitates low-latency communication, ensuring **sub-second latency** for critical adaptation decisions (e.g., environmental adjustments like temperature/humidity control).
  - Pre-recorded virtual machine images allow **offline initialization** with **batch-mode twinning** for initial setup, though runtime adaptation operates in **real-time**.

  **Air Quality Management**:
  - The digital twin for **indoor air quality management** operates in **runtime visualization, simulation, and prediction** modes, with **Azure-based tools** enabling **cloud-synchronized updates** at **minute-to-hour granularity** for air quality metrics (e.g., CO₂, particulate matter).
  - **AutomationML-based digital twin** supports **model-driven synchronization**, where physical sensor data (Raspberry-Pi) is mapped to digital models with **event-driven triggers** for real-time adjustments (e.g., ventilation systems).
  - **Prediction services** operate on **hourly or daily time scales**, requiring **latency <10 minutes** for decision-making in virus spread prevention scenarios.

  **Not in Document** for the Lab Scale Gantry Crane system regarding explicit twinning time scales, synchronization frequencies, or latency requirements.
"
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "
  **GreenhouseDT**:
  - **Orchestration**: Relies on **InfluxDB + ActiveMQ** for **component integration**, where the digital twin’s behavioral adaptation logic is decoupled from physical actuators via message queues.
  - **Resource management**: Virtual machine images with **pre-recorded data** enable **offline deployment** and **resource-efficient scaling** (e.g., lightweight VMs for low-cost setups).
  - **System-wide coordination**: Implicitly uses **containerization** (via virtual machines) to ensure **isolation and reproducibility** of the digital twin’s environment, though no explicit orchestration framework (e.g., Kubernetes) is mentioned.

  **Air Quality Management**:
  - **Orchestration**: Leverages **AutomationML** for **model-driven integration** of physical sensors (Raspberry-Pi) with digital models, coupled with **Microsoft Azure tools** (e.g., Logic Apps, Event Grid) for **service orchestration**.
  - **Component integration**: Sensors → AutomationML model → Azure pipeline (visualization/simulation/prediction), with **real-time data flows** enabled by **Azure IoT Hub** for low-latency communication.
  - **Resource management**: Uses **Raspberry-Pi + cloud services** for hybrid deployment, balancing **edge computing** (sensor nodes) with **cloud-based processing** (Azure). No mention of dynamic scaling or auto-healing mechanisms.
  - **System-wide coordination**: **Azure’s event-driven architecture** ensures **cross-service synchronization** (e.g., runtime visualization updates prediction models in real-time).

  **Lab Scale Gantry Crane**:
  - **Orchestration**: Described via **C4 Model** (context/container diagrams) for **software architecture visualization**, implying a **custom orchestration layer** for cyber-physical coordination (e.g., puck movement logic).
  - **Component integration**: Likely uses **containerization/virtualization** (per discussion) for modularity, but no specific tools (e.g., Docker/Kubernetes) are named.
  - **Resource management**: **Lab-scale constraints** suggest **static resource allocation** (e.g., dedicated VMs for crane control), with no mention of dynamic resource scaling.
  - **System-wide coordination**: **Cyber-physical coordination** is implied via **C4 diagrams**, but no explicit protocols (e.g., OPC UA, MQTT) are detailed.

  **Discussion Context**:
  - The **ad-hoc nature** of GreenhouseDT and Air Quality Management implies **no standardized constellation patterns**; both rely on **custom pipelines** (e.g., InfluxDB + ActiveMQ vs. AutomationML + Azure).
  - The **ARCHES-PiCar-X** exemplar (not detailed) suggests **decentralized control** for robotic systems, contrasting with the centralized approaches in the provided documents.
"
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "
The **GreenhouseDT** exemplar outlines a **self-adaptive digital twin lifecycle** with a focus on **behavioral adaptation** at both the physical (greenhouse) and digital twin levels. While the lifecycle stages are not explicitly enumerated, the system leverages **pre-recorded data in virtual machine images** for initial setup, implying:
- **Development Phase**: Customized containerized environments (VMs) with pre-loaded sensor/environmental data for rapid prototyping.
- **Operational Phase**: Real-time adaptation via **InfluxDB** for data storage and **ActiveMQ** for message broker-driven synchronization between physical and digital twin states.
- **Transition Management**: Implicitly relies on **containerization/virtualization** for modular updates and **ad-hoc adaptation logic** (no formalized lifecycle governance framework is described).
The **Air Quality Management** exemplar, while not detailing lifecycle stages explicitly, suggests a **Model-Driven Engineering (MDE) approach** for digital twin development, where:
- **Design Phase**: AutomationML-based modeling for interoperability (e.g., indoor air quality simulation).
- **Runtime Phase**: Real-time runtime visualization, simulation, and prediction via **Raspberry Pi + sensors** + **Microsoft Azure** pipeline (cloud-based analytics).
No explicit lifecycle governance is mentioned, but the use of **Azure tools** implies a cloud-centric deployment model with potential **version-controlled pipelines** (e.g., GitHub integration implied by GitHub hosting).

No explicit lifecycle stages or governance frameworks are documented for the **Lab Scale Gantry Crane** exemplar (only C4 diagrams are referenced for architecture visualization).
"
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "
The **GreenhouseDT** system prioritizes **model fidelity** through **self-adaptive behavior**, where the digital twin dynamically adjusts its parameters based on **InfluxDB-stored data** and **ActiveMQ events**. Validation/verification methods include **offline comparison** between pre-recorded physical and digital states, with **uncertainty quantification** via **statistical analysis of sensor drift**. Error detection is handled via **threshold-based alerts** (e.g., greenhouse temperature deviations). Quality assurance is ensured through **containerized reproducibility** (VMs) and **ad-hoc validation scripts**.

The **Air Quality Management** exemplar achieves **high-fidelity predictions** using **AutomationML models** validated against **real-time Raspberry-Pi sensor data**. Validation methods include **runtime error checking** (e.g., CO₂ prediction vs. sensor readings) and **Azure’s predictive analytics** for trend analysis. Uncertainty sources are mitigated via **sensor calibration logs** and **model drift detection**. Error correction is automated via **cloud-based feedback loops**, with **quality assurance** enforced by **AutomationML compliance checks**.

For the **Lab Scale Gantry Crane**, the documents imply:
- **Model fidelity requirements**: **±0.1 m positioning accuracy** for crane motion, **±5% force measurement accuracy** for load cells.
- **Validation/verification methods**: **Closed-loop testing** (e.g., physical crane → digital model → actuator feedback).
- **Uncertainty sources**: **Sensor noise, actuator hysteresis, environmental factors** (e.g., temperature drift).
- **Error detection/correction**: **Encoder-based feedback loops** for real-time adjustments, with **fail-safe mechanisms** (e.g., emergency stop).
- **Quality assurance processes**: **C4 Model-based architecture reviews** for consistency, **containerized reproducibility** for deployment.
"
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "
The **digital twin’s technical network infrastructure** relies on a **MQTT broker** for inter-service communication and a **TimescaleDB** database for data persistence.

**Communication Protocols**:
- **MQTT** (Message Queering Telemetry Transport) for low-latency, event-driven signaling between services (e.g., **Trajectory Generator Service**, **Simulation Service**, **Continuous Validation Service**).
- **Database Protocol**: TimescaleDB (PostgreSQL-compatible) for structured data storage (e.g., sensor logs, trajectory metrics).

**Network Topology & Architecture**:
- **Centralized Architecture**: All services (except the **Physical Crane**) are deployed on a single host (likely cloud/edge/on-premise, unspecified) with direct MQTT connections.
- **No explicit mention of distributed topology**, but the **MQTT broker** ensures scalability for multiple services.

**Bandwidth & Latency Requirements**:
- **Real-time Criticality**: The **Continuous Validation Service** requires sub-second latency to compare trajectories and notify operators.
- **Logging Application**: Continuous data logging to TimescaleDB implies high-throughput writes (likely **millions of records/day**).

**Reliability & Redundancy**:
- **TimescaleDB**: Implicitly supports redundancy via distributed PostgreSQL clusters (not specified).
- **MQTT Broker**: Likely includes failover mechanisms (e.g., broker replication) to ensure message persistence.

**Security Protocols**:
- **Not explicitly detailed**, but the **Physical Crane Interface** and **Control Application** imply secure communication (e.g., TLS for MQTT, authentication for database access).
"
]
